shape_decode: cmp,vp,vpc,naive = L,_,W,L : value_profile_cmplog RESOLVES while cmplog AND naive BLOCK and value_profile is non-decisive. Single-technique vp-PRO: the decisive delta over cmplog is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient, and because plain value_profile is non-decisive the gradient operates only in conjunction with I2S via vpc.

hypotheses.label: vpc's CMP_MAP gradient ratchets the corpus byte-by-byte toward a CONCRETE literal token that cmplog's edge-only signal cannot bank near-miss progress toward

measurement.registry_tool_rationale: BUILT tool value_distance_reached (descriptor_key seed_set:value_distance_reached:operand_literal:per_branch) directly measures per-arm MIN Hamming distance to a known operand literal. Fits the multi-byte literal-token climb (operand width>=2): 'xml:id'(6), 'ID'(2), '0x'(2). joint_necessity does NOT fit (it scores a JOINT assembly-depth subtype and reports vp_tags over a value_profile-BLOCKING seed set, but value_profile is NON-DECISIVE here). operand_enrichment does NOT fit (fixed-offset byte presence, not graded Hamming distance).

metric_definitions.w_min_hamming_to_literal: over the vpc-RESOLVING (W) seed set, the median per-seed minimum Hamming distance between the gate-literal-width window scanned across the seed and operand_literal (normalized 0..1 by operand_width_bytes)
metric_definitions.l_min_hamming_to_literal: same statistic over the cmplog/naive-BLOCKING (L) seed set
metric_definitions.distance_gap: l_min_hamming_to_literal - w_min_hamming_to_literal (positive => W seeds sit closer to the literal than L seeds)

hypotheses.prediction: W seeds sit measurably CLOSER to the gate literal than L seeds (distance_gap > 0 AND w_min_hamming_to_literal low), because the CMP_MAP gradient preserved near-miss seeds inching toward the literal. This is DISCRIMINATING: the competing 'I2S substitution alone suffices' alternative predicts cmplog-BLOCKING seeds would be EQUALLY close (one splice from the literal, so distance_gap ~ 0) since both arms share the I2S mutator; observing a real gap rules that alternative out and confirms the gradient (not I2S) is what banks the progress.

hypotheses.label: vpc's CMP_MAP gradient accumulates partial credit across chained comparisons to drive the corpus to a structurally DEEPER parse state (multi-child content model / namespace binding / internal subset / pointer-identity) whose gate is a derived pointer/state check with NO fixed literal pre-image

measurement.registry_tool_rationale: BUILT tool depth_reach (descriptor_key seed_set:depth_reach:branch_line:per_branch) runs W vs L seeds through the libafl-<target>-cov image and reads the branch line's per-seed-normalized execution + blocked-side count. Fits the parse-depth-reached question for gates with operand_literal=null. value_distance_reached cannot apply (no byte-comparable literal: runtime-interned pointer or accumulated parse state). Retained as the depth-axis test; the structural_token_assembly hypothesis below is the seed-stat (non-Docker) companion that REUSES a built tool and is scoreable without the cov image.

metric_definitions.w_depth: median structural depth reached by the vpc-RESOLVING (W) seed set = count of nested/chained structural tokens on the gate's parse path (e.g. completed second-child element, namespace declaration, internal-subset bracket nesting) per seed
metric_definitions.l_depth: same statistic over the cmplog/naive-BLOCKING (L) seed set
metric_definitions.depth_lift: w_depth / l_depth (>1 => W corpus reaches a structurally deeper parse state than L)

depth_token_per_branch.libxml2_6654: two-child content group (both cur and last non-NULL)
depth_token_per_branch.libxml2_7451: namespaced element with matched xmlns prefix binding (prefix==pref)
depth_token_per_branch.libxml2_7459: namespace-resolution path with non-NULL URI binding

hypotheses.prediction: W seeds reach a structurally DEEPER parse state than L seeds (depth_lift > 1, w_depth meaningfully above l_depth), because the gradient banked credit on each chained comparison to climb the multi-token structure. This is DISCRIMINATING: the competing 'literal-token climb' alternative (sibling hypothesis) predicts a fixed-distance byte signature and NO depth difference (l seeds already at the same structural depth, just one literal short); observing a depth lift with NO scoreable single literal rules the literal-climb explanation out and confirms a structural-assembly mechanism. It is also INDEPENDENT of the resolve pattern (G2): parse depth is a property of the seed bytes, not of which fuzzer saved them.

hypotheses.label: vpc's CMP_MAP gradient enriches the saved corpus with the target byte at a FIXED file offset on a single-byte enumerated-constant gate, where a Hamming-distance climb is degenerate (1-byte distance is binary) but a corpus byte-presence enrichment is graded
hypotheses.novelty_vs_refuted: Genuinely different MEASUREMENT than the value_distance_climb that scored rule_not_met on this branch: at width=1 the operand literal Hamming distance is binary (0 or 1), so distance_gap cannot grade near-miss progress (the registry flags width<=2 as chance-alignment prone). This pivots to operand_enrichment, which scores the FRACTION of the saved corpus carrying the target byte (0x03) vs the decoy byte (0x06) at the fixed IHDR offset 0x19 as a cmplog-vs-naive log2 enrichment — a different statistic on a different axis, not a relaxed-threshold re-test.

measurement.registry_tool_rationale: BUILT tool operand_enrichment (descriptor_key corpus_queue:byte_freq_ratio:target_vs_decoy:per_gate_offset, anchor harfbuzz/5391, validated pro median +3.12) is exactly a fixed-offset single-byte VALUE-gate enrichment test. libpng/3999 is a fixed-offset (IHDR color_type byte at file offset 0x19) single-byte equality (target 0x03=PALETTE vs decoy 0x06=RGBA) — a perfect fit. It is the right tool precisely where value_distance_reached degenerates (1-byte Hamming).

metric_definitions.signed_target_enrich: log2 enrichment of the target byte (0x03) at the gate offset in the cmplog/vpc resolving corpus vs the naive baseline; positive => the gradient drove the corpus to carry the target value
metric_definitions.decoy_enrich: log2 enrichment of the decoy byte (0x06) at the same offset; the L (blocking) arm should retain the decoy

hypotheses.prediction: The resolving corpus is ENRICHED for the target byte 0x03 at offset 0x19 (signed_target_enrich > 0) while the decoy 0x06 is NOT enriched there. This is DISCRIMINATING: the competing 'I2S substitution alone suffices' alternative predicts cmplog (I2S, no gradient) would enrich the target byte just as much as vpc — i.e. signed_target_enrich would not separate the gradient arm; observing a positive enrichment that tracks the value_profile arm confirms the CMP_MAP gradient, not I2S, is what concentrates the rare 0x03 PALETTE value. It is INDEPENDENT of the resolve pattern (G2): byte frequency at a fixed offset is a property of the saved bytes, not of which fuzzer saved them.

hypotheses.label: vpc's CMP_MAP gradient assembles a LARGER, MORE structural-token-dense input than cmplog leaves behind, climbing a multi-token grammar (DTD content model / namespaced start-tag / interned attribute name) whose gate is satisfied by structural depth rather than a single byte-comparable literal
hypotheses.novelty_vs_refuted: Genuinely different MEASUREMENT than both refuted siblings: (a) value_distance_reached scored rule_not_met for libxml2/6726 ('xmlns') because the gate is really a pointer-identity check (attname==ctxt->str_xmlns) that requires a well-formed start-tag assembly, not a free-floating literal one splice away; (b) the depth_reach companion needs the libafl-<target>-cov Docker image and is BLIND without seeds. This pivots to the BUILT joint_necessity tool in its generic analyze_pair mode (winner=value_profile_cmplog RESOLVING vs loser=cmplog BLOCKING), which emits size_lift and tag_lift over XML structural tokens WITHOUT needing a value_profile-blocking arm (so vp_tags being undefined is irrelevant) and WITHOUT Docker — a pure seed-stat measurement on a different axis (assembly extent + token density) than Hamming distance.

measurement.registry_tool_rationale: BUILT tool joint_necessity (descriptor_key seed_set:struct_size_and_token_count:tokens:per_branch) has a generic analyze_pair(winner_fz, loser_fz, tokens) mode that compares a vpc-RESOLVING seed set to a cmplog-BLOCKING seed set and emits winner_tags/loser_tags/tag_lift/size_lift — it does NOT require a value_profile-blocking arm, so the vp_tags-undefined obstruction (value_profile non-decisive in this shape) does NOT apply. This is the BUILT-tool companion to the depth_reach descriptor and is the right pivot for assembly-depth gates that have no byte-comparable literal. --tokens is target-specific: XML structural tokens for libxml2.

metric_definitions.size_lift: median(vpc-RESOLVING seed size) / median(cmplog-BLOCKING seed size); >1 => the gradient assembled a larger input than I2S-only reached
metric_definitions.tag_lift: median structural-token count of the vpc-RESOLVING seeds minus that of the cmplog-BLOCKING seeds (winner_tags - loser_tags); >0 => W carries more grammar tokens
metric_definitions.vpc_tags: structural-token count of the vpc-RESOLVING (W) seed set
metric_definitions.cmp_tags: structural-token count of the cmplog-BLOCKING (L) seed set

hypotheses.prediction: The vpc-RESOLVING seeds are LARGER and carry MORE XML structural tokens than the cmplog-BLOCKING seeds (size_lift > 1 AND tag_lift > 0), because the gradient banked credit across chained grammar comparisons to assemble a deep multi-token structure (nested content group / namespaced start-tag / internal subset) that I2S-only never built. This is DISCRIMINATING: the competing 'literal-token climb' alternative predicts W and L seeds are the SAME structural size (size_lift ~ 1) and differ only by one missing literal (tag_lift ~ 0, no extra grammar tokens); observing a size+token lift rules the single-literal-climb explanation out and confirms structural assembly. The competing 'I2S already assembled enough' alternative predicts size_lift ~ 1 too (cmplog seeds already as large), which the same rule excludes. INDEPENDENT of the resolve pattern (G2): seed size and token count are properties of the bytes, not of which fuzzer saved them.

hypotheses.label: vpc's CMP_MAP gradient assembles a DISTINCTIVE multi-byte gate keyword/token that is PRESENT in the vpc-resolving corpus but DEPLETED in the cmplog-blocking corpus — a token-presence signature (does the saved corpus carry the literal at all?) rather than a Hamming-distance climb or a fixed-offset byte-frequency enrichment
hypotheses.novelty_vs_refuted: Genuinely DIFFERENT mechanism+measurement than every test already refuted for these two branches. (1) value_distance_reached (Hamming climb) scored rule_not_met / was inapplicable: 6673's gate operand is a SINGLE byte ('N' switch dispatch, width=1 => binary Hamming, degenerate) and 6726's gate is a pointer-identity check (attname==ctxt->str_xmlns) with no free-floating literal one splice away. (2) operand_enrichment is inapplicable: neither token sits at a FIXED file offset (6673's NOTATION keyword follows a variable-position '<!' markup token; 6726's 'xmlns' sits at a parse-state-dependent attribute-name position) => no_gate_signature. (3) joint_necessity size_lift/tag_lift scored rule_not_met for 6726 in round-1 (the discriminating signal is presence of ONE distinctive token, not aggregate structural-token density / bigger seed). This pivots to the BUILT token_count tool, which COUNTS occurrences of the exact distinctive literal ANYWHERE in each arm's seeds (no fixed offset needed) and reports the loser/winner presence ratio — a categorical PRESENCE axis, orthogonal to graded distance, fixed-offset byte-frequency, and structural-token-density. The cards state the discriminating fact directly: for 6673 the cmplog-blocking corpus 'never synthesizes the <!N notation token' (only <!E/<!A) while the winner takes case 'N'; for 6726 cmplog 'never assembles the exact interned token at the attribute-name position' while the winner forms a literal 'xmlns' attribute name. token_count tests exactly that presence-vs-absence claim.

measurement.registry_tool_rationale: BUILT tool bench/tools/token_count.py (branch mode --literal L --winner-fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --loser-fuzzer cmplog) counts the mean per-seed occurrences of the exact distinctive gate literal in the vpc-RESOLVING (resolving_seeds) corpus vs the cmplog-BLOCKING (blocking_seeds) corpus, and reports literal_presence_ratio = (loser_count+eps)/(winner_count+eps). It reads seed bytes anywhere (NO fixed file offset, unlike operand_enrichment) and counts an EXACT literal (NOT a graded Hamming distance, unlike value_distance_reached; NOT aggregate token density, unlike joint_necessity). Descriptor key seed_set:token_count:literal:per_branch is built but not yet catalogued in tool_registry.json tools[] — orchestrator should register it.

metric_definitions.winner_literal_count: mean per-seed occurrences of the gate literal in the vpc-RESOLVING (resolving_seeds) corpus
metric_definitions.loser_literal_count: mean per-seed occurrences of the gate literal in the cmplog-BLOCKING (blocking_seeds) corpus
metric_definitions.literal_presence_ratio: (loser_literal_count + 0.5) / (winner_literal_count + 0.5); < 1 => the distinctive token is DEPLETED in the cmplog-blocking arm relative to the vpc-resolving arm

params.literal_choice_rationale: 'NOTATION' (8 bytes) is the distinctive keyword the case 'N' arm dispatches into (xmlParseNotationDecl) — far less false-positive-prone than the single byte 'N' or 3-byte '<!N'; the cards confirm the loser exhausts ELEMENT/ATTLIST but never the NOTATION declaration. 'xmlns' (5 bytes) is the exact interned attribute-name token the gate compares against (attname==ctxt->str_xmlns); the loser never forms it.

hypotheses.prediction: The distinctive gate literal is PRESENT in the vpc-resolving corpus (winner_literal_count >= 1) and DEPLETED in the cmplog-blocking corpus (literal_presence_ratio << 1), because only the CMP_MAP gradient banked near-miss progress to finish the multi-byte token. This is DISCRIMINATING: the competing 'I2S substitution alone suffices' alternative predicts the literal would be EQUALLY present in BOTH arms (both share the I2S mutator, so cmplog could splice it too => literal_presence_ratio ~ 1, winner_literal_count comparable to loser); observing the token in the winner but ABSENT from the loser rules that alternative out and confirms the gradient, not I2S, is what assembled it. It is also DISCRIMINATING vs the structural-assembly sibling (which predicts a size/tag-density lift but NOT necessarily a single-token presence gap) and INDEPENDENT of the resolve pattern (G2): literal occurrence count is a property of the saved bytes, not of which fuzzer saved them.

hypotheses.label: vpc resolves a single-byte dispatch/terminator gate whose required byte sits at a PARSE-STATE-DEPENDENT (non-fixed-file) position — a terminator following a variable-length name — where no built tool can separate the CMP_MAP-gradient mechanism from its alternatives
hypotheses.non_discriminable_rationale: libxml2/6696 is a single-byte terminator gate (RAW != ';' at xmlParsePEReference): the false branch needs the byte after a variable-length PE name to be exactly 0x3b (';'). No BUILT measurement can discriminate the gradient mechanism here: (1) value_distance_reached is degenerate at width=1 (Hamming distance binary, no graded climb) — already scored rule_not_met; (2) operand_enrichment needs a FIXED file offset, but ';' must follow a variable-length '%name', so there is NO constant gate offset — returns no_gate_signature; (3) joint_necessity size_lift/tag_lift does NOT apply — a single terminator byte is not a structural-assembly depth, and ';' is an extremely COMMON XML byte (statement/declaration/entity terminators throughout), so a token_count presence test on ';' would be DOMINATED by background occurrences in both arms (literal_presence_ratio ~ 1 regardless of mechanism — non-discriminating). The mechanism (the CMP_MAP gradient steering toward the ';' terminator) is plausible from the card but is honestly NON-DISCRIMINABLE with the current built toolset. Per G3 left inconclusive rather than forced under a measurement that cannot separate the mechanism from its alternatives. libxml2/6673 ('N'/NOTATION) was moved OUT of this group into vp_gradient_literal_token_presence because its downstream NOTATION keyword IS a distinctive, low-false-positive token that token_count can score; ';' has no such distinctive companion token.
hypotheses.prediction: decidable:false — no falsifiable, discriminating real-data prediction is supported by a built tool for a single-byte terminator gate at a parse-state-dependent position whose only candidate token (';') is a high-frequency XML byte present in both arms.

data_realities_respected: No descriptor uses seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence on an 'I2S event' or a 'gradient event' — value_profile is a FEEDBACK invisible in mutation_op, so its effect is measured only via what the corpus REACHES/ADMITS (Hamming-distance-to-literal, parse depth, fixed-offset byte enrichment, assembly size+token density, exact-token PRESENCE), never via a lineage event.

value_distance_reached is applied ONLY to multi-byte literal gates (width>=2: 'xml:id','ID','0x'); the single-byte FIXED-offset gate (libpng/3999, width=1) uses operand_enrichment; the single-byte STREAM-POSITION gates that DO have a distinctive downstream keyword (6673 -> NOTATION) use token_count's exact-PRESENCE axis; the single-byte stream-position gate with only a high-frequency companion byte (6696 -> ';') has no fixed offset AND no distinctive token, so it is decidable:false.

joint_necessity/struct_size_and_token_count is used in analyze_pair (vpc-RESOLVING vs cmplog-BLOCKING) mode, which needs NO value_profile-blocking arm — so it remains valid even though value_profile is non-decisive (`_`) in this shape and vp_tags would be undefined. It respects that joint/assembly gates have different-SIZED W/L seeds (byte-alignment / byte_freq_ratio would fail there), so it measures size_lift+tag_lift instead.

token_count (bench/tools/token_count.py) reads seed bytes ANYWHERE in the seed (no fixed offset), counting an EXACT distinctive literal — so it is valid for parse-state-dependent token positions where operand_enrichment (fixed-offset) returns no_gate_signature. It is applied ONLY to LOW-FALSE-POSITIVE distinctive tokens ('NOTATION','xmlns'); never to common single bytes like ';' (which would be background-dominated and non-discriminating).

CROSS-SERVER: libpng and libxml2 corpora live on sB (lcms,libxml2,libpng,bloaty); sqlite3 on s4. operand_enrichment (libpng/3999), value_distance_reached, joint_necessity, depth_reach and token_count over libxml2/sqlite3 seeds are arbitrated by the OWNING server — branches are NOT marked decidable:false merely because the running server lacks the corpus. Each server scores only its own targets.

depth_reach needs the libafl-<target>-cov Docker image + W/L seeds; the structural_token_assembly (joint_necessity) hypothesis is the non-Docker, BUILT-tool companion that can score the same structural set from seed stats alone where the cov image / depth tool is unavailable.
notes: ROUND-2 RE-DESIGN (loop-until-dry). SUPERSET/MONOTONIC: all FIVE prior hypotheses retained verbatim so the 6 CONFIRMED branches re-validate — (1) vp_gradient_value_distance_climb [KEEP_CONFIRMED, 4 literal members 6268/6621/14233/14786 + the 'ANY' keyword branch 6663 which the arbiter scored confirmed under the same Hamming rule]; (2) vp_gradient_structural_depth_assembly [KEEP, depth_reach, Docker depth axis]; (3) vp_gradient_fixedoffset_byte_enrichment [KEEP_CONFIRMED, libpng/3999 via operand_enrichment]; (4) vp_gradient_structural_token_assembly [KEEP, joint_necessity size_lift/tag_lift]. NEW MECHANISM for unlabeled branches: (5) vp_gradient_literal_token_presence [libxml2_6673, libxml2_6726] reuses the previously-UNTRIED BUILT tool token_count — a genuinely different PRESENCE axis (does the vpc-resolving corpus carry the distinctive gate keyword that the cmplog-blocking corpus lacks?) orthogonal to the already-refuted Hamming-distance (6673 single-byte degenerate; 6726 pointer-identity), fixed-offset byte-frequency (neither token at a fixed offset), and structural-token-density (6726 size_lift/tag_lift failed in round-1) tests. Literals chosen for low false-positive distinctiveness: 'NOTATION'(8) for 6673 (the keyword behind case 'N'), 'xmlns'(5) for 6726 (the interned attribute-name token). DECIDABLE:FALSE (G3, honest inconclusive): (6) vp_gradient_streamposition_single_byte_dispatch now holds ONLY libxml2_6696 (';' terminator) — single-byte, parse-state-dependent position, and its only candidate token ';' is a high-frequency XML byte that would dominate any token_count test in BOTH arms (non-discriminating); value_distance_reached degenerate at width=1, operand_enrichment inapplicable (no fixed offset). The 5 structural-assembly branches (6649_h0/6654/7352/7451/7459) REMAIN under hypotheses (2)+(4) per the superset rule — if the arbiter again scores them rule_not_met under both depth_reach AND joint_necessity, they fall to fallback:inconclusive honestly (the gradient drives a derived pointer/parse-state gate with no byte-comparable literal and no aggregate size/token-density signal the built tools can separate from background). UNTRIED-TOOL PIVOT USED: token_count (-> hypothesis 5). corpus_size_ratio NOT used: it is a WHOLE-CORPUS, BRANCH-INDEPENDENT measure (same numbers for every branch of the value_profile_cmplog/cmplog pair), so it cannot discriminate AMONG these per-branch gradient mechanisms, and its inflation/homogenization hypothesis is a non-local corpus-scale story that does not match the local per-gate gradient mechanism the cards describe — proposing it would violate G1 (non-discriminating). FEASIBILITY: hypotheses 1/3/4/5 reuse BUILT tools (value_distance_reached, operand_enrichment, joint_necessity, token_count all under bench/tools/); hypothesis 2 (depth_reach) needs the tool + cov image; all seed-dependent and arbitrated by the corpus-owning server. PRIORS KEPT: vp_gradient_value_distance_climb, vp_gradient_structural_depth_assembly, vp_gradient_fixedoffset_byte_enrichment, vp_gradient_structural_token_assembly (all verbatim). NEW: vp_gradient_literal_token_presence. DECIDABLE:FALSE: libxml2_6696 (and, as honest fallback if both structural tests fail again, 6649_h0/6654/7352/7451/7459).
